habitat: Primary lowland and hill forest; dipterocarp forest; favoring areas with ridges
story: An icon of the skies, the Bornean Peacock Pheasant (Polyplectron schleiermacheri) is a species from the Phasianidae family. It thrives in Primary lowland and hill forest; dipterocarp forest; favoring areas with ridges, where it interacts with its environment in unique ways. With a conservation status of Endangered (EN), the Bornean Peacock Pheasant is a focus for conservationists worldwide. Understanding the needs of the Bornean Peacock Pheasant helps us better comprehend the challenges facing our planet's wildlife. This poster is a celebration of the Bornean Peacock Pheasant, a bird that inspires wonder and highlights the critical importance of conservation efforts.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
